blocker: The proposed v16 insertion is directionally correct but cannot be adopted unchanged because existing normative clauses would still conflict with it, child subtitle/context attribution is not unified, fallback acceptance is incomplete, and the component's v14/v15 version history is not transaction-ready.
rationale: The common projection, arbitrary-DAG, orthogonality, additive-JSON, hermetic quota, and no-live-provider directions match the approved requirements. The recommended title allowance of 3 concurrent workers (hard max 4) and 4 starts per rolling 60 seconds (hard max 4) is conservative relative to the current 2 concurrent and 3 starts/60 seconds. Adoption is blocked by unresolved contradictions and missing exact tests/version repairs listed below.

required_fixes.location: proposed F-37a versus prd.md F-17/F-33 and render layout contracts
required_fixes.issue: The proposal adds a common detail row but does not explicitly supersede the current wide-only NOW clause or the existing inline wide/narrow/stack context gauges. Applying it additively permits duplicate context and contradictory wide-only behavior.
required_fixes.fix: State that F-37a replaces the affected F-17 and F-33 rendering clauses. Define exactly one subordinate detail row for wide, narrow, and stack, immediately after the complete identity card and before the sub-agent strip, with context immediately before fresh NOW. Pin the truth table: live context+NOW; live context without NOW; live missing context with NOW as `ctx — · NOW`; live missing both as `ctx —`; stale/dead entity omits the whole detail row regardless of cached values. Remove or explicitly retire duplicate inline context rendering.
evidence: prd.md:197 says the subtitle is wide-only and silent when absent
prd.md:344 keeps the expanded context gauge wide-only and says narrow/stack are unchanged
tools/fleet/render.py:1001-1004,1433-1438,1455-1478 render context inside existing rows/cards
tools/fleet/render.py:2955-2958 emits session NOW only on the wide path

required_fixes.location: proposed F-37b and existing F-32 child title/summary adoption
required_fixes.issue: F-37b strengthens only the context join to `(pid,proc_start)`, while the current title/NOW join uses PID alone. The new combined detail row could therefore pair exact context from one child with a reused-PID subtitle from another child.
required_fixes.fix: Define one child-session association that supplies title, NOW, and context together. Use exact `(pid,proc_start)` first; allow `(harness,realpath(cwd))` only when exact identity is unavailable and exactly one child candidate exists. Refuse PID reuse, duplicate PID candidates, cwd ambiguity, and cross-harness matches. Never inherit parent context. Add a PID-reuse fixture proving subtitle and context are both withheld rather than mixed.
evidence: tools/fleet/collectors/__init__.py:63-94 indexes title/summary by PID without proc_start
Session and DispatchJob already carry proc_start in tools/fleet/model.py
existing cwd ambiguity tests cover title/summary but not PID reuse or combined context

required_fixes.location: proposed F-36b/F-36d versus prd.md F-28a/F-28 lock
required_fixes.issue: The new fail-closed rule conflicts with the current normative rule that missing, unparsable, or hash-invalid route records quietly fall back to legacy heuristics. The meanings and field-level authority of `route-exact` and `registry-exact` are also not fully defined.
required_fixes.fix: Explicitly supersede F-28's heuristic fallback whenever any explicit route tuple exists. Define that a verified record plus exact entity tuple may populate topology, progress, and node state; exact registry/env evidence without a verified record may preserve observed identity fields but may not fabricate validated topology, progress, gate passage, or node completion. Any explicit conflict or validation failure must remain unknown/ambiguous and must not fall through to artifacts. Preserve artifact inference only when route evidence is completely absent.
evidence: prd.md:320 requires quiet pipe-heuristic fallback on record absence/parse/hash failure
prd.md:523 locks tolerant fallback regression behavior
tools/fleet/route.py:489-497 currently emits a heuristic view when a route_id is known but the record is unavailable

required_fixes.location: v16 acceptance-test matrix: artifact fallback and ambiguity
required_fixes.issue: The matrix checks exact-over-artifact conflicts but does not prove the artifact-only boundary itself: exactly one plan directory, zero candidates, multiple candidates, or the prohibition on synthesizing route/gate/node completion from artifacts.
required_fixes.fix: Add fixtures for one exact plan-dir candidate (`source=artifact-inferred`, stage_label only), zero candidates (`source=none`), two or more exact/fuzzy candidates (`source=none` plus ambiguity evidence), and an explicit invalid route tuple coexisting with a plausible plan directory (unknown/ambiguous, never artifact-derived). Assert route_id, route_node, progress, gate, and completion remain absent on all artifact paths.
evidence: tools/fleet/collectors/dispatch.py:675-744 currently chooses exact suffix or a best token-overlap candidate
tools/fleet/collectors/dispatch.py:1449-1456 currently re-derives stage for working registry rows

required_fixes.location: proposed F-39 versus prd.md F-17/F-23 and locked F-23
required_fixes.issue: The proposed limits are acceptable, but adding F-39 alone leaves multiple older normative budgets active and leaves the custom-provider no-tools enforcement boundary implicit.
required_fixes.fix: Replace or mark superseded every current numeric budget clause: F-17 child/shared limit text, F-23 hard-limit/rolling-window/acceptance text, and the locked F-23 paragraph. Make F-39 the single current source of truth for 3 concurrent (max 4), 4 starts/60 seconds (max 4), 600/150-second debounces, shared cross-process leases, stale-lease recovery, and both kill switches. State that Fleet enforces shell-free argv and the default provider's explicit tool denial; a custom `FLEET_TITLE_COMMAND` wrapper is responsible for enforcing its own no-tools property, which Fleet cannot introspect.
evidence: prd.md:198 still states concurrency 2 and 16 starts in a 600-second budget
prd.md:228-231 and prd.md:500 still specify older 2/4/16/600-second behavior
tools/fleet/refresh_title.py currently uses 2 concurrent (max 4), 3 starts/60 seconds (max 3), and 600/150-second debounces
tools/fleet/refresh_title.py:259-283 explicitly disables tools only for the default provider; custom argv is shell-free but wrapper-owned

required_fixes.issue: The matrix says decreasing telemetry becomes unknown, but a lower current context percentage can be legitimate after compaction. This condition is absent from F-38 and unsupported by the current normalization policy.
required_fixes.fix: Change the assertion to missing, stale, malformed, or source-sequence-regressed telemetry becoming unknown. Do not treat a numeric decrease alone as invalid; use timestamp/cursor ordering to reject an older sample if that is the intended case.
evidence: tools/fleet/token_budget.py policy_band classifies the current normalized percentage without a monotonicity rule
proposed F-38 names missing/stale/malformed, not decreasing, as unknown conditions

required_fixes.location: component version transaction and pipeline metadata
required_fixes.issue: The current PRD header contains v15, but local snapshots stop at v13 and pipeline_state.yaml/pipeline_summary.md still describe v14. The transaction helper therefore computes v14 as the next snapshot, so a direct v16 update would mis-version or lose the current v15 delta. Pipeline state would also falsely retain dev=done for unimplemented v16 work.
required_fixes.fix: Under the canonical spec lock, first repair the missing local v14 snapshot from the authoritative v14 copy at `.agent_reports/spec/_internal/versions/v21/agent-fleet-dashboard/prd.md`. Then run the v16 component update as a second locked transaction: snapshot the current component PRD verbatim to `_internal/versions/v15/prd.md`, write v16 from that current v15 base, and update the v16 header, section, locked-decision block, version history, implementation/acceptance next steps, pipeline_state.yaml, and pipeline_summary.md together. Mark v16 spec done but v16 development pending/not done, and record the carried v15 memory-sync delta rather than reverting to v14.
evidence: component `_internal/versions/` contains v1..v13 with no v14 or v15
utilities/spec-transaction.py computes next_version as max snapshot + 1
the authoritative nested v21 snapshot is v14 and differs from current component PRD only by the v15 memory-sync edits
prd.md mtime is later than pipeline_state.yaml and pipeline_summary.md; both metadata files still say v14

required_fixes.location: prd.md module structure and component diagram
required_fixes.issue: v16 introduces a shared WorkProjection as the only render/JSON input, but the fixed module tree and Mermaid diagram still route Session/DispatchJob directly to render.py and name no projection owner.
required_fixes.fix: Update the module ownership and component diagram in the same PRD transaction. Name the single resolver location (for example model.py or a dedicated projection.py) and show Session plus DispatchJob evidence flowing through WorkProjection before group view, process view, plain `--once`, and JSON serialization. Keep the resolver source-agnostic and read-only.
evidence: prd.md:388-410 declares the module structure fixed
prd.md:415-446 shows collectors -> Session/DispatchJob -> render with no common projection layer

recommendations.recommendation: Adopt the research limits of default concurrency 3 (hard max 4) and 4 starts per rolling 60 seconds (hard max 4) after RF-05 is applied.
recommendations.rationale: This is a bounded one-step increase over the current defaults, permits a three-slot cold fill plus at most one replacement in the same minute, and preserves cross-process fail-closed leases and the global pool. No live-provider smoke is needed or desirable.

recommendations.recommendation: Use stable machine-readable ambiguity codes such as `route-record-mismatch`, `multiple-leaf-candidates`, `multiple-child-cwd-candidates`, and `multiple-artifact-plan-dirs` inside `ambiguity[]`.
recommendations.rationale: Stable codes make JSON assertions and operator diagnosis less dependent on prose.

recommendations.recommendation: Keep all quota tests hermetic with a fake clock and isolated state root, and fail the test if either `subprocess.Popen` or provider `subprocess.run` is reached from snapshot, demo, width, projection, or storm fixtures.
recommendations.rationale: This preserves deterministic lease/window tests and the explicit no-live-provider acceptance boundary.

recommendations.recommendation: Retain canonical-to-Claude Fleet mirror parity in the implementation verification set in addition to the v16 acceptance matrix.
recommendations.rationale: The existing repository treats the mirror as a compatibility surface; the new model and render contract will touch mirrored files.

qa_policy_evidence.independence: This dispatched research/plan-review stage is the independent deep-review pass; it did not dispatch another worker.
qa_policy_evidence.final_verification: 179 selected baseline tests passed after correcting an initial unittest module-name invocation; these tests validate current repository behavior and transaction tooling, not unimplemented v16 behavior.

unsupported_runtime_contracts: The spec-read marker side effect could not write under `/home/Uihyeop/agent_setting/.spec-grounding` because that worker-visible location is read-only; the PRD itself was read successfully.

No live default or custom title provider was invoked. The proposed 3/4 quota behavior was assessed from repository lease logic and hermetic tests, not measured against a live provider or quota service.
A custom `FLEET_TITLE_COMMAND` provider's no-tools property cannot be enforced or introspected by Fleet beyond the shell-free argv contract; that property remains the wrapper's responsibility.

The required post-write `file-write` heartbeat was attempted after the baseline `test` heartbeat and the registry rejected the phase transition as `progress-phase-regression` (`test->file-write`). The durable artifact is instead finalized with the allowed terminal artifact phase.
